description: The image displays a crystal structure model of Fe-Mn-Al alloys, showing a cubic supercell with B2 ordered structure (space group Pm-3m). The ball-and-stick representation illustrates different atomic species occupying specific lattice sites: purple spheres represent Fe atoms, gray spheres represent Al atoms, and green spheres represent Mn atoms in the substitutional solid solution. This structural model represents the computational supercell used in first-principles DFT calculations (CASTEP code with GGA-PBE, 450 eV cutoff, 8×8×8 k-points) to investigate nine compositions ranging from FeAl to MnAl (Fe1-xMnxAl, x = 0 to 1). The visualization demonstrates the atomic arrangement and lattice symmetry that underpins the calculated electronic structures, formation enthalpies, elastic constants (C11, C12, C44), and mechanical properties (bulk modulus, shear modulus, Young's modulus) reported in the study. The model specifically supports the investigation of how Mn substitution affects the thermodynamic stability, bonding character (covalent/metallic mix), and elastic anisotropy of the B2-phase Fe-Mn-Al system.
